methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $5.0B.

data.narrative:
Kraftblock is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Martin Schichtel and Susanne König, having raised €20M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of six same-sector, same-stage private companies, supplemented by one public comparable (ticker LNZA). The primary peer cohort includes Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), reflecting significant dispersion across the climate tech landscape.

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given data availability. The median valuation across the six private comparables stands at $355M, with a range of $120M to $5.0B. This wide dispersion—driven in part by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' outsized valuation—suggests material heterogeneity in investor assessment of stage, technology maturity, and market timing within the sector. The peer-median result of $355M implies a valuation floor and serves as the central anchor.

Based on peer-median triangulation, the implied valuation range is $302M to $408M, with a median of $355M. Key uncertainties center on Kraftblock's positioning relative to outlier peers (particularly CFS) and whether comparable metrics such as capital efficiency, technology risk, or market traction justify placement toward the lower or upper quartile of the observed range. Additional data on unit economics, customer concentration, and technology stage would refine this estimate.
